message.content:
你是调研 finder。任务背景:MAformac 项目(纯端侧 macOS SwiftUI 演示助手,主演示面已定为 macOS,后续要做 UIUE 前置化)。
要求:
- 先用 ToolSearch 加载 WebSearch 和 WebFetch,然后联网搜索。
- 每条 findings 的 claim 必须来自你真实打开(WebFetch)或搜索摘要确认过的来源;禁止凭记忆编造 URL、数字、版本号。能 WebFetch 的关键来源尽量实访,并把 fetched 标 true。
- 优先 2024-2026 的新资料(Liquid Glass / macOS 26 时代),老经典(HIG 长期原则、Nielsen 启发式)也可收但注明。
- 输出全部用中文(URL/API 名/专有名词保留英文)。
- 8-15 条高质量 findings,宁缺毋滥。
调研角度:AI 辅助 UI 开发工作流——用 LLM/agent 生成与迭代 SwiftUI 界面的方法(截图驱动迭代、snapshot 对比、agent 自动评审 UI)、AI 时代的 design-to-code 工具链现状、这类工作流的已知坑(幻觉 API、风格漂移)与对策。

attachment.content:
- archive-research-pack: Use when 一次多路 workflow 调研 / deep-research / pre-mortem oracle 搜证收口,需要把成果落盘防失忆。症状:调研完只想存一个总报告、transcript 在 /tmp 待清会丢、finder 引的 source URL 事后无法复核、各路一手档与综合报告混在一起。
- axiom-accessibility: Use when fixing or auditing ANY accessibility issue — VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, color contrast, touch targets, WCAG compliance, App Store accessibility review.
- axiom-ai: Use when implementing ANY Apple Intelligence or on-device AI feature. Covers Foundation Models, @Generable, LanguageModelSession, structured output, Tool protocol, iOS 26 AI integration.
- axiom-apple-docs: Use when ANY question involves Apple framework APIs, Swift compiler errors, or Xcode-bundled documentation. Covers Liquid Glass, Swift 6.2 concurrency, Foundation Models, SwiftData, StoreKit, 32 Swift compiler diagnostics.
- axiom-build: Use when ANY iOS build fails, test crashes, Xcode misbehaves, or environment issue occurs before debugging code. Covers build failures, compilation errors, dependency conflicts, simulator problems, environment-first diagnostics.
- axiom-concurrency: Use when writing ANY async code, actors, threads, or seeing ANY concurrency error. Covers Swift 6 concurrency, @MainActor, Sendable, data races, async/await patterns.
- axiom-data: Use when working with ANY data persistence, database, storage, CloudKit, migration, or serialization. Covers SwiftData, Core Data, GRDB, SQLite, CloudKit sync, file storage, Codable, migrations.
- axiom-design: Use when making design decisions, implementing HIG patterns, Liquid Glass, SF Symbols, typography, or structuring app entry points and authentication flows.
- axiom-games: Use when building ANY 2D or 3D game with SpriteKit, SceneKit, or RealityKit, or adding touch controls or game controller support. Covers scene graphs, ECS, physics, actions, game loops, rendering, TouchController, GCController.
- axiom-graphics: Use when working with ANY GPU rendering, Metal, OpenGL migration, shaders, 3D content, RealityKit, AR, USD/USDZ files, or display performance. Covers Metal migration, shader conversion, RealityKit ECS, RealityView, USDKit, ProMotion.
- axiom-health: Use when working with HealthKit, WorkoutKit, health data, workouts, or fitness features on iOS or watchOS. Covers permissions, queries, background delivery, custom workouts, multidevice coordination.
- axiom-integration: Use when integrating ANY iOS system feature - Siri, Shortcuts, widgets, IAP, localization, privacy, alarms, calendar, reminders, contacts, background tasks, push notifications, timers. Covers App Intents, WidgetKit, StoreKit, EventKit, Contacts.
- axiom-location: Use when implementing location services, maps, geofencing, or debugging location/MapKit issues. Covers Core Location, CLMonitor, MapKit, annotations, directions.
- axiom-macos: Use when building ANY macOS app — windows, menus, sandboxing, distribution, AppKit bridging or modernization (control events, state restoration, concentric corners), or macOS-specific SwiftUI patterns.
- axiom-media: Use when working with camera, photos, audio, haptics, ShazamKit, or Now Playing. Covers AVCaptureSession, PHPicker, PhotosPicker, AVFoundation, Core Haptics, audio recognition, MediaPlayer, CarPlay, MusicKit.
- axiom-networking: Use when implementing or debugging ANY network connection, API call, or socket. Covers URLSession, Network.framework, NetworkConnection, connection diagnostics.
- axiom-payments: Use when accepting ANY real-world payment — Apple Pay, Wallet passes, Tap to Pay, Orders in Wallet. NOT in-app purchase / digital content (use axiom-integration). Covers entitlements, certs, signing, App Review payment rules.
- axiom-performance: Use when app feels slow, memory grows, battery drains, or diagnosing ANY performance issue. Covers memory leaks, profiling, Instruments workflows, retain cycles, performance optimization.
- axiom-security: Use when storing credentials securely, encrypting data, implementing passkeys, securing AI/agentic features against prompt injection, code signing, or managing certificates and provisioning profiles.
- axiom-shipping: Use when preparing ANY app for submission, handling App Store rejections, writing appeals, or managing App Store Connect. Covers submission checklists, rejection troubleshooting, metadata requirements, privacy manifests, age ratings, export compliance.
- axiom-swift: Use when reviewing Swift code for modern idioms, working with noncopyable types, implementing drag and drop, adding debug deep links, or building for tvOS.
- axiom-swiftui: Use when building, fixing, or improving ANY SwiftUI UI — views, navigation, layout, animations, performance, architecture, gestures, debugging, iOS 26 features.
- axiom-testing: Use when writing ANY test, debugging flaky tests, making tests faster, or choosing Swift Testing vs XCTest. Covers unit tests, UI tests, async testing, test architecture.
- axiom-tools: Use when asking how to use Axiom or what skills exist, capturing console with xclog, symbolicating .ips/MetricKit/.crash crashes with xcsym, driving/validating simulator UI & accessibility with xcui, or analyzing xctrace/CPU profiles with xcprof.
- axiom-uikit: Use when bridging UIKit and SwiftUI, modernizing UIKit apps (scene lifecycle, resizability), debugging Auto Layout, Combine, TextKit, PencilKit, or UIKit animations.
- axiom-vision: Use when implementing ANY computer vision feature — image analysis, pose detection, person segmentation, subject lifting, text recognition, barcode scanning.
- axiom-watchos: Use when building ANY watchOS app — app structure, independent apps, Watch Connectivity, Smart Stack widgets, complications, controls, RelevanceKit, background tasks, ClockKit migration.
- axiom-xcode-mcp: Use when connecting to Xcode via MCP, using xcrun mcpbridge, or working with ANY Xcode MCP tool (XcodeRead, BuildProject, RunTests, RenderPreview). Covers setup, tool reference, workflow patterns, troubleshooting.
- closeout-receipt-writer: Use when 一个 apply/重型任务/审计 wave 收口需要写 receipt 证据。症状:想用 hardcoded pass、读 metadata flag 自证 verdict、凭印象写状态、或 receipt 与实跑 log 脱节;0-34 灾难根因正是 receipt 读自己的 metadata flag 翻 pass、零 token 证据。
- doc-cascade-sweep: Use when 范式翻案/架构大改/口径回写后,grep 全仓命中几十个文件含过期锚点或旧数字,需要全仓级联更新。症状:看到 grep 命中 60+ 文件就想 sed 全量替换、或一个决策只 append 进一个文件没回写文档组、或改了带日期的历史快照正文破坏一手溯源。
- ios-debugger-agent: Build, run, and debug iOS apps on Simulator with XcodeBuildMCP. Use when launching an app, inspecting simulator UI or logs, or diagnosing runtime behavior.
- ios-ettrace-performance: Capture and interpret iOS Simulator ETTrace profiles. Use when profiling launch or runtime latency, comparing traces, or finding CPU-heavy stacks.
- ios-simulator-skill: 29 production-ready scripts for iOS app testing, building, and automation. Provides semantic UI navigation, build automation, accessibility testing, and simulator lifecycle management. Optimized for AI agents with minimal token output.
- openspec-apply-change: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Use when the user wants to start implementing, continue implementation, or work through tasks.
- openspec-archive-change: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow. Use when the user wants to finalize and archive a change after implementation is complete.
- openspec-explore: Enter explore mode - a thinking partner for exploring ideas, investigating problems, and clarifying requirements. Use when the user wants to think through something before or during a change.
- openspec-propose: Propose a new change with all artifacts generated in one step. Use when the user wants to quickly describe what they want to build and get a complete proposal with design, specs, and tasks ready for implementation.
- openspec-sync-specs: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs. Use when the user wants to update main specs with changes from a delta spec, without archiving the change.
- verify-external-claims: Use when 一个多路 workflow/finder 调研、deep-research、或综合官报告引用了 arxiv ID、精确数字（调用数/百分比/star/benchmark）、或 repo 活跃度断言,且这些要驱动决策。症状:finder 在搜证压力下高发编造精确数字 + 引用 ID,且常安到真实存在的 source 上(issue/论文真、数字假),综合官即使做了本机 grep 也漏外部声称。
- opsx:apply: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change (Experimental)
- opsx:archive: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow
- opsx:explore: Enter explore mode - think through ideas, investigate problems, clarify requirements
- opsx:propose: Propose a new change - create it and generate all artifacts in one step
- opsx:sync: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about AI design-to-code tools for Figma to SwiftUI conversion in 2025-2026:

## Key Tools and Approaches

The Figma to SwiftUI tooling category has matured significantly since 2024, with seven serious options ranging from free official Figma exports to paid SaaS with AI assistance.

### Notable Solutions:

1. **Codia AI** - Their Figma plugin Design2Code now supports direct conversion to SwiftUI, marking a significant step in streamlining the design-to-code process.

2. **Claude AI Integration** - A comprehensive approach combining cutting-edge AI assistance with hands-on development, leveraging Claude 3.5 Sonnet to efficiently generate and refine SwiftUI code.

3. **Builder.io Visual Copilot** - Builder.io ships an AI mapping tool that converts Figma designs to SwiftUI using your existing component library, with the AI doing the mapping while your component library stays the source of truth.

4. **Other Options** - For free quick exports use Figma Code Connect or the built-in SwiftUI Code Generator, while for paid pixel perfect output use Locofy Lightning or Anima for Figma.

## Important Limitations on Hallucination

AI assisted converters handle nesting better but introduce hallucinated property names. Additionally, none of the tools fully solve nested Auto Layout or interactive states, with users expected to spend 20 to 50 percent of design time refining the output.

Tools are expected to save 50 to 80 percent of typing, not 100 percent.

I found relevant search results on this topic. Here's what I discovered:

## Apple's UICoder: LLM Self-Teaching SwiftUI Design

Apple researchers developed UICoder, an LLM that self-teaches SwiftUI UI design through iterative self-critique, generating syntactically correct and aesthetically pleasing code despite scarce training data.

The approach involves a screenshot-driven feedback loop. The model was given UI descriptions and instructed to generate a massive synthetic dataset of SwiftUI programs from those descriptions. Then, every piece of code was run through a Swift compiler to ensure it compiled, followed by an analysis by GPT-4V, a vision-language model that compared the compiled interface with the original description. Any outputs that failed to compile, looked irrelevant, or were duplicates, were discarded. The remaining outputs formed a high-quality training set.

This process was repeated multiple times, with each iteration showing that the improved model generated better SwiftUI code than before, which in turn fed into an even cleaner dataset. After five rounds, they had nearly one million SwiftUI programs (996,000 to be precise).

## Visual Feedback for UI Iteration

Beyond Apple's work, screenshots and browser context can be used as inputs for AI-assisted iteration. By pairing screenshots with prompts and augmenting context via browser-based MCPs, developers can give AI direct visibility into what's wrong and receive precise, design-aligned suggestions for improvement.

There's also research on scaffolded app generation: In 25 minutes, all 12 participants were able to generate a complete, multi-screen app with navigation flows that could run on a real device, with developers using the approach building functional apps with twice as many views and three times as much code in the same amount of time compared to a baseline chat-based LLM interface.

input.prompt: Extract specifics on Apple's UICoder: the training loop (compiler feedback, GPT-4V/vision model screenshot comparison), dataset sizes/numbers, number of iterations, accuracy/compilation rate improvements, and any concrete methodology for screenshot-driven UI code iteration. Give exact numbers.

content.content:
# Apple's UICoder: Key Technical Specifications

## Training Loop Methodology
The iterative process involved two automated feedback mechanisms:

1. **Swift Compiler Verification**: "every piece of code through a Swift compiler to make sure it actually ran"
2. **Vision Model Evaluation**: GPT-4V analyzed compiled interfaces by comparing them against original UI descriptions, filtering out failures, irrelevant outputs, and duplicates

## Dataset Scale
- **Final dataset**: 996,000 SwiftUI programs generated across iterations
- **Starting model**: StarChat-Beta (open-source coding LLM)
- **Iterations completed**: 5 rounds of refinement

## Performance Improvements
- UICoder "significantly outperformed the base StarChat-Beta model on both automated metrics, and human evaluations"
- **Compilation success rate**: Exceeded GPT-4's performance
- **Overall quality**: Approached GPT-4 parity

## Critical Context
The original StarChat-Beta training data contained minimal SwiftUI examples—"Swift code repositories were excluded by accident" from TheStack dataset, and only contained "one example (out of ten thousand)" SwiftUI code in OpenAssistant-Guanaco. This absence meant UICoder's gains derived from self-generated, validated datasets rather than rehashing existing training data.

The methodology reportedly generalizes to "other languages and UI toolkits" beyond SwiftUI.

# Figma-to-SwiftUI Tools Comparison (2026)

## The Seven Tools

| Tool | Function | Key Limitation |
|------|----------|----------------|
| **Figma SwiftUI Code Generator** | Built-in inspector export; no installation | "Each layer becomes a separate ZStack with absolute frame positioning" |
| **Figma Code Connect** | Maps Figma components to existing SwiftUI code; surfaces snippets | Only works for pre-built components; cannot generate novel layouts |
| **Trace Plugin** | Exports with smarter Auto Layout inference than built-in tool | "Output still requires cleanup for production" |
| **Locofy Lightning** | AI + tagging system marks layers as components/buttons; generates production code | Assumes UIKit interop on certain components |
| **Anima for Figma** | Plugin-based SwiftUI export with typography/spacing focus | Less aggressive AI cleanup than competitors |
| **Builder.io Visual Copilot** | AI maps Figma designs using existing component library as source of truth | Requires dashboard configuration; $19/month minimum |
| **Designcode with Claude** | Manual JSON export + Claude conversation for AI conversion | Manual workflow; best output requires skillful prompting |

## Manual Refinement Required

The article states: "plan to spend 20 to 50 percent of design time refining the output" and "Expect tools to save 50 to 80 percent of typing, not 100 percent."

Time-to-production estimates range from 15–45 minutes (Locofy) to 2–4 hours (built-in generator).

## Critical Unsolved Problems

Five categories remain manual: state management (@State/@Binding), navigation flows, networking/APIs, persistence (SwiftData/Core Data), and accessibility (VoiceOver labels, Dynamic Type).

## AI Hallucination Note

The article mentions "AI assisted converters handle nesting better but introduce hallucinated property names"—identifying a known reliability issue with AI-generated code.

# Visual Feedback Loop Methodology for AI-Assisted UI Development

## Core Process

The workflow follows a three-phase cycle:

**1. Screenshot Capture**
Developers capture visual artifacts using browser tools or extensions, targeting: full-page views, component sections, or specific device viewports. These images serve as direct inputs to AI systems.

**2. Context-Enriched Analysis**
Rather than relying solely on images, the methodology recommends augmenting context through Browser MCPs that extract:
- Console logs and error messages
- DOM structure and element properties
- Network activity and failed API calls

As the guide states, this approach allows AI to reason about "layout bugs, render issues, or API failures" by combining visual and technical signals.

**3. Iterative Refinement**
After AI analysis, developers prompt the model to "generate safe changes step-by-step," then validate with new screenshots before repeating the cycle.

## Key Practices

- **Be specific:** Reference concrete problems ("font size too large on buttons," "image overflowing container") rather than vague complaints
- **Use annotations:** Leverage tool-native markup features when available
- **Validate progress:** Capture screenshots after each iteration to confirm improvements align with design intent

## Primary Use Cases

Apply this workflow when validating newly-generated UI, reviewing QA-reported visual bugs, or refactoring components for cross-browser compatibility.

The methodology emphasizes that screenshots "make invisible problems visible" by surfacing spacing, color, font, and layout inconsistencies that code review alone might miss.

I've completed a web search on snapshot testing, SwiftUI, AI visual regression agents, VLM UI review, and automated testing in 2025. Here's what I found:

## Key Findings

**Snapshot Testing for SwiftUI:**
Snapshot testing provides automated visual regression detection, and it's a software testing method that ensures your UI does not have unexpected changes (regressions).

**AI-Powered Visual Regression Agents:**
Percy launched its AI-powered Visual Review Agent in late 2025, which reduces review time by 3x and automatically filters out 40% of false positives. Additionally, TestMu AI provides agent-skills that can generate visual regression configurations automatically, interpreting prompts like "run visual regression on login and homepage across browsers".

**VLM (Vision Language Model) Integration:**
Recent VLMs enable direct visual grounding from raw screenshots, substantially improving cross-platform generalization through technologies like CogAgent and SeeClick for accurate UI element localization and action prediction.

**Market Growth:**
The AI-enabled testing market was valued at $1.01 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $4.64 billion by 2034.

The search results show active development in AI-driven visual testing tools and integration with SwiftUI frameworks for automated UI validation.

I've completed the web search on AI-generated code style drift, consistency, design system enforcement, component libraries, and source of truth. Here are the key findings:

## Key Issues with AI-Generated Code and Design Systems

AI breaks design systems when it generates UI without access to actual components, tokens, and usage rules, forcing it to guess from statistical averages of training data.

### Types of Drift Problems

Within-session drift occurs when the same component creates three slightly different paddings because the model loses values from previous messages. Between-session amnesia means each new chat fabricates different tokens for the same components, causing Monday's and Wednesday's builds to silently disagree.

## Solutions and Best Practices

Teams must define governance before defining components, since without a governance model, teams add components ad-hoc, tokens drift, and documentation falls behind within months.

Design systems must be machine-readable using structured data formats rather than static images or PDFs, with clear naming conventions that APIs or MCP servers can access.

Enforcement mechanisms like linting token usage and blocking non-system values in pull requests turn consistency intent into actual constraints—when AI-generated code flows through this loop, consistency becomes measurable.

Token naming conventions, component documentation format, and design-to-code parity all affect how well AI tools like Cursor, Claude Code, and Figma Make work within the system.

content.content:
# Athena: Intermediate Representations for Iterative App Generation

## Core Methodology

Athena uses three intermediate representations (IRs) to scaffold LLM-driven multi-screen app generation:

1. **Storyboard**: A directed graph representing screens and navigation flows between them
2. **Data Model**: Swift structs defining data entities displayed or manipulated across screens
3. **GUI Skeletons**: SwiftUI pseudocode specifying layout, UI elements, and interactions per screen

## How Scaffolding Reduces Errors

Rather than generating complete code from a single prompt, Athena decomposes the process into ordered steps that respect dependencies. The system modifies IRs sequentially—Storyboard first, then Data Model, then GUI Skeletons—ensuring each representation remains internally consistent. This cascading approach prevents hallucination by constraining what the LLM can generate at each stage.

The authors note that "choosing to revisit earlier stages...can still be costly," but the intermediate checkpoints allow developers to catch errors before code generation.

## User Study Results (12 Participants, 25 Minutes)

- **Complexity**: Athena apps averaged **6.0 views** (SD=2.2) vs. baseline's **3.1 views** (SD=1.1)
- **Code volume**: **353.9 lines** (SD=92.8) vs. baseline's **117.8 lines** (SD=36.8)
- **Preference**: **75% preferred Athena** for prototyping from initial ideas; **100% preferred it** for navigation flows
- **Satisfaction**: Athena scored 3.42/5 vs. baseline's 4.0/5 (latency and bugs cited)

## Why Scaffolding Helps

Participants reported that IRs "matched...how I would build an app," enabling them to iterate on navigation before implementation details. One developer noted: "if the interaction was...I get an Xcode project...I would need to understand like all of it at once. Whereas by breaking it down...I can be more confident."

This progressive disclosure reduced cognitive load while giving developers control over generated output before compilation.

# AI Design System Drift: Concrete Mechanisms and Fixes

## Types of Drift

**Within-Session Drift**
The model loses consistency mid-build. Example: "Same component, three uses, three slightly different paddings" across a single conversation.

**Between-Session Amnesia**
The model forgets previous decisions. "Whatever it figured out yesterday is gone. A fresh chat fabricates different tokens for the same components."

**Token Fabrication**
The model invents plausible-sounding tokens that don't exist in the actual system, e.g., using `--color-primary-500` when the system defines `--brand-action-bg`.

**Silent Breaking Changes**
The model continues using old component props after they've been renamed, because "nothing told it the system moved."

## Root Cause

"AI breaks your design system when it generates UI without access to your actual components, tokens, and usage rules" (AutonomyAI). The model reconstructs unspecified parts at each generation, creating divergence rather than consistency.

## Machine-Readable Constraints

**DESIGN.md File**
A frozen token reference file containing colors, spacing, type, and radius values that the agent reads but never regenerates. The critical rule: "If a token you need does not exist, stop and ask. Do not invent one."

**Component APIs**
Constraining the model to "assemble your real components instead of inventing layouts" prevents hand-rolled duplicates with incorrect props.

## Verification Mechanisms

- **Linting**: Scanning for "raw hex and off-system elements"
- **Screenshot Loop**: "Render it, screenshot it, compare against the reference, because the model cannot see its own output"
- **Render-Compare Validation**: Automated visual comparison to catch divergence before shipping

## Key Methodological Claim

"The durable fix is 'context engineering,' a canonical project state plus locked anchors plus component assembly, not a single document you paste and forget. The file is the floor. The loop is the fix."

content.content:
# AI Agent QA Workflow for UI Regression Testing

## Agent Exploration Mechanism

Agents navigate applications by learning flows rather than following static paths. They're seeded with "routes, a sitemap, or Storybook stories" and provided credentials for different roles. The exploration uses a "planner [that] reads the DOM, picks actionable elements by role and visibility, and triggers state transitions," with memory tracking to avoid revisiting states.

## Visual Signals & Capture

Agents use multiple signal types:
- **Screenshots**: Captured at defined checkpoints when "layout shifts settle"
- **DOM analysis**: OCR fallback when "shadow-root soup" prevents semantic understanding
- **Layout stability scoring**: Inspired by Core Web Vitals, snapshots trigger only when movement "drops below a tiny threshold"

The comparison engine employs visual diffing tools (Percy, Applitools, BackstopJS) using "SSIM, perceptual diffs, and layout-aware checks" rather than pixel-by-pixel comparison.

## False Positive Control

The workflow masks dynamic regions through "CSS or selector-based ignore areas" and sets "thresholds by page type: 0.1% area difference or SSIM < 0.98 for forms." Environment control is critical—pinned browser versions, frozen timezone, stubbed network requests, and disabled animations in test mode reduce noise.

## Loop Steps

1. Ephemeral environment setup per PR
2. Agent-driven state exploration with guardrails (data-testids, metadata on destructive actions)
3. Screenshot capture at stability checkpoints
4. Visual diff comparison against baseline
5. Triage workflow: "fix, accept baseline, or ignore"

## Key Metrics

- **False positive rate**: Target under 15%
- **Median triage time**: Under 10 minutes (50th percentile)
- **Escaped regressions**: One B2B SaaS team achieved "62% reduction in two releases" after implementing agent-driven testing
- **Coverage**: The same team tested "180 critical flows"

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Apple Xcode 26 and its AI-powered coding intelligence features:

## Xcode 26 Coding Intelligence Overview

Xcode 26 includes a fully functional version of Coding Intelligence, an AI-powered coding companion for a wide variety of coding tasks. Coding Intelligence is a collection of features that allow you to write code, understand unfamiliar code bases, refactor existing code, generate new code, and generate documentation for your code.

## SwiftUI Preview and AI Integration

Xcode's agentic coding features can capture actual screenshots of SwiftUI previews, allowing agents to literally see what the UI looks like, change a color, verify visually, and iterate without leaving the agent workflow. Agents can search documentation, explore file structures, update project settings, and verify their work visually by capturing Xcode Previews and iterating through builds and fixes.

## Addressing AI Hallucination and API Issues

While the search results discuss extensive features for AI-powered coding in Xcode 26, they emphasize grounding through documentation integration. Xcode sends system instructions and file contents to the LLM, including the current file context before making requests. This context-aware approach helps mitigate hallucinations by providing the model with actual project code and documentation.

Early reports suggest that the on-device model struggles with complex SwiftUI view hierarchies, often suggesting older iOS 15-style modifiers instead of newer iOS 18/19 conventions, which is attributed to "training data lag" in on-device models.

The search results don't contain specific information about dedicated hallucination mitigation strategies or special API grounding documentation features beyond the context-aware approach mentioned above.

content.content:
# Xcode 26.3 Agentic Coding: SwiftUI Iteration Capabilities

Based on the Apple newsroom article, here's what's offered for SwiftUI UI iteration:

## Visual Verification Loop

The article states agents can "verify their work visually by capturing Xcode Previews and iterating through builds and fixes." This enables a feedback loop where agents take screenshots of preview renders and use them to make iterative improvements.

## Integrated Agents

Two agents have direct integration:
- **Anthropic's Claude Agent**
- **OpenAI's Codex**

The document does not mention Cursor or Claude Code by name. However, it notes broader extensibility through the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## MCP Support

"Xcode 26.3 makes its capabilities available through the Model Context Protocol, an open standard that gives developers the flexibility to use any compatible agent or tool with Xcode."

## Workflow Details

The article provides limited concrete workflow specifics but indicates agents can:
- Search documentation
- Explore file structures
- Update project settings
- Capture Xcode Previews
- Iterate through builds and fixes

The document emphasizes autonomy but doesn't detail the exact interaction patterns developers would use.

# Xcode 26.3 Agentic Coding: SwiftUI Preview Screenshots & Visual Verification

## Visual Preview Capture Mechanism

Xcode 26.3 introduces the `RenderPreview` tool, which uniquely enables agents to obtain actual screenshots of SwiftUI previews. As the article notes, "This returns actual screenshots of your SwiftUI previews. Your agent can literally _see_ what your UI looks like."

This capability distinguishes Xcode from competitors: "No other IDE offers this to external agents." Agents can modify UI code, capture the resulting preview image, and verify changes without manual inspection.

## The Visual Iteration Loop

The workflow operates as a feedback cycle. An agent modifies color values or layout properties, invokes `RenderPreview`, examines the screenshot, and either confirms the change meets requirements or iterates further. The article emphasizes this removes friction: "Change a color, verify visually, iterate — all without leaving the agent workflow."

## Core Agentic Tools Available

Twenty MCP tools expose Xcode capabilities across five categories:

- **Build & Test**: `BuildProject`, `GetBuildLog`, `RunAllTests`, `RunSomeTests`, `GetTestList`
- **Diagnostics**: Issue detection and code refresh
- **File Operations**: Read, write, update, and filesystem navigation
- **Intelligence**: `DocumentationSearch` (WWDC transcripts included), `ExecuteSnippet` (Swift REPL)
- **Workspace**: `XcodeListWindows` for context discovery

## Workflow Best Practice: Context Discovery

Agents automatically discover which Xcode window to operate on before acting. The article illustrates this: "Agent: I'll first list open Xcode windows to get the tab identifier... → XcodeListWindows()." This eliminates manual tab specification overhead.

## Project-Level Guidance

Apple recommends repository-root files (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`) to provide project context, helping agents understand build systems, testing conventions, and framework usage without explicit instruction each interaction.

## Known Limitations

A spec compliance gap exists: `mcpbridge` omits `structuredContent` responses required by the MCP specification, causing Cursor to reject tool outputs. This limitation affects third-party clients but not Apple's co-designed integrations (Claude Code, Codex).

Web search results for query: "LLM hallucinate SwiftUI API nonexistent modifier grounding documentation retrieval mitigation 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Mitigating LLM Hallucinations through Domain-Grounded Tiered Retrieval","url":"https://arxiv.org/html/2603.17872v1"},{"title":"How to Reduce LLM Hallucinations | Zep","url":"https://www.getzep.com/ai-agents/reducing-llm-hallucinations/"},{"title":"LLM Hallucinations in AI Code Review | diffray","url":"https://diffray.ai/blog/llm-hallucinations-code-review/"},{"title":"De-Hallucinator: Mitigating LLM Hallucinations in Code Generation Tasks   via Iterative Grounding","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2401.01701"},{"title":"LLM Hallucinations in 2026: How to Understand and Tackle AI’s Most Persistent Quirk | Lakera – Protecting AI teams that disrupt the world.","url":"https://www.lakera.ai/blog/guide-to-hallucinations-in-large-language-models"},{"title":"Hallucination Mitigation for Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models: A Review","url":"https://www.mdpi.com/2227-7390/13/5/856"},{"title":"On Mitigating Code LLM Hallucinations with API Documentation","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2407.09726"},{"title":"An Empirical Analysis of Static Analysis Methods for Detection and Mitigation of Code Library Hallucinations","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.07755"},{"title":"GitHub - EdinburghNLP/awesome-hallucination-detection: List of papers on hallucination detection in LLMs. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/EdinburghNLP/awesome-hallucination-detection"},{"title":"A Systematic Literature Review of Code Hallucinations in LLMs: Characterization, Mitigation Methods, Challenges, and Future Directions for Reliable AI","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.00776"}]

The search has returned several highly relevant results on LLM hallucinations and mitigation strategies. Here's what I found:

## Key Findings

**LLM Hallucinations in Code Generation:**

API hallucinations occur in various software engineering contexts where LLMs produce incorrect or nonexistent API references. This is particularly relevant to your SwiftUI query, as the problem extends to framework-specific APIs.

**Documentation Retrieval Mitigation:**

Documentation Augmented Generation (DAG) significantly improves performance for low frequency APIs, and intelligent triggering of DAG using API indexes or confidence scores can help retrieve only when needed. Additionally, the De-Hallucinator approach reduces hallucinations by iteratively retrieving relevant API references to ground the model.

**General Mitigation Strategies (2025):**

Grounding, abstention, verification, and constrained outputs each cut different classes of errors, with retrieval fixing factuality issues. The most promising systems add span-level verification where each generated claim is matched against retrieved evidence, and best practice combines RAG with automatic span checks.

**Recent Research:**

2024-2025 research has identified mitigation strategies that can reduce hallucinations by up to 96%—but no tool eliminates them entirely. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhances handling of domain-specific knowledge, though limitations within RAG components may still cause hallucinations.

The search results contain multiple 2025-2026 papers specifically addressing API hallucinations and documentation-based mitigation strategies for code generation tasks.

# SwiftUI Snapshot Testing Methodology

Based on the provided content, here's what the article covers:

## Core Concept
The article frames snapshot testing as a solution for "visual bugs that traditional testing misses," positioning it as an automated visual regression detection system that captures reference images for comparison.

## SwiftUI-Specific Challenges Mentioned
The guide addresses: "Environment value management (`.colorScheme`, `.sizeCategory`), State testing with `@State` and `@ObservableObject`, Layout constraints for consistent rendering, ViewInspector integration patterns."

## Reference Implementation
The article provides this example structure:
```swift
assertSnapshot(
    matching: collapsed, 
    as: .image(layout: .fixed(width: 300, height: 80)), 
    named: "collapsed"
)
```

This demonstrates basic snapshot syntax with fixed layout dimensions and named references.

## Testing Scope
The methodology covers: "Multi-device testing strategies, Accessibility validation (Dynamic Type, high contrast), Complex view hierarchy testing, Performance optimization."

## Notable Limitation
The article doesn't specify tolerance settings, perceptual precision configurations, flakiness mitigation strategies, or how snapshots serve as AI training ground truth. The content focuses on production workflows and team integration rather than technical snapshot internals.

**Library**: The article references snapshot testing generally but links to a Medium guide for specific implementation details not fully detailed in this excerpt.

input.angle: AI 辅助 UI 开发工作流——用 LLM/agent 生成与迭代 SwiftUI 界面的方法、design-to-code 工具链现状、已知坑（幻觉 API / 风格漂移）与对策
input.summary: 对 MAformac（纯端侧 macOS SwiftUI 演示助手）最直接可迁移的核心机制是「截图驱动的视觉反馈闭环」：让 agent 修改 SwiftUI 代码后渲染截图、由 vision 模型/人对照参考图判优、再迭代——Apple 自研 UICoder 用「Swift 编译器过一遍 + GPT-4V 对照描述筛图」的自监督循环，5 轮迭代生成约 99.6 万份 SwiftUI 程序，编译通过率超过 GPT-4，证明该闭环能把「看不见的视觉问题变可见」。工程侧 Xcode 26.3 已把这个闭环产品化：新增 RenderPreview MCP 工具让外部 agent（Claude Agent / OpenAI Codex）直接抓取 SwiftUI Preview 截图「亲眼看 UI」，配 20 个 MCP 工具（BuildProject/RunAllTests/DocumentationSearch 等），实现「改颜色→视觉验证→迭代」不出 agent 工作流——这对 MAformac 的 macOS SwiftUI 卡片式 mock UI 迭代是现成基础设施。已知两大坑：一是幻觉 API（编造不存在的 SwiftUI modifier/属性名，on-device 模型还倾向老 iOS 15 写法），对策是文档接地（DAG/RAG 检索真实 API + 编译门 fail-closed）；二是风格漂移（session 内三处 padding 不一致、跨 session 编造不同 token），对策是「机器可读的冻结 token 参考 + 组件 API 装配而非现造 + 渲染截图对比参考图」的 context engineering。Design-to-code 工具（Locofy/Builder.io/Codia/Anima）已成熟但仍需 20-50% 人工精修，state/导航/网络/持久化/无障碍五类仍是手工区。中间表示（Athena 的 Storyboard→Data Model→GUI Skeleton 分级脚手架）与 snapshot 测试可作为约束生成与回归防线。这些都指向：对 MAformac 应把「编译门 + 截图对照门 + 冻结设计 token」做成机械闭环，而非靠 prompt 自律。

findings.claim: Apple UICoder 用自监督截图闭环训练 SwiftUI 生成：每份生成代码先过 Swift 编译器确认能跑，再由 GPT-4V 视觉模型对照原始 UI 描述判断是否相符，剔除编译失败/不相关/重复项；如此迭代 5 轮，最终得到约 996,000 份 SwiftUI 程序，编译通过率与整体质量超过/逼近 GPT-4。这验证了「编译门 + 视觉对照门」是提升 AI 生成 UI 质量的核心机制。
findings.source_title: Apple trained an LLM to teach itself good UI code in SwiftUI - 9to5Mac
findings.notes: 起点模型 StarChat-Beta，原因是训练语料几乎无 SwiftUI（TheStack 误排除 Swift 仓，OpenAssistant-Guanaco 万里挑一）；方法据称可泛化到其他语言/UI 工具包。对 MAformac：可借鉴用编译门+截图对照做生成质量门。

findings.claim: Xcode 26.3 把「截图驱动视觉迭代」产品化：新增 RenderPreview MCP 工具，让外部 agent 直接拿到 SwiftUI Preview 的真实截图「亲眼看 UI」，官方称『No other IDE offers this to external agents』；配合共 20 个 MCP 工具分五类（Build&Test: BuildProject/RunAllTests/RunSomeTests；Intelligence: DocumentationSearch 含 WWDC transcript、ExecuteSnippet REPL；File/Workspace/Diagnostics），实现「改颜色→RenderPreview 看截图→确认或再迭代，全程不出 agent 工作流」。
findings.source_title: Xcode 26.3: Use AI Agents from Cursor, Claude Code & Beyond - DEV Community
findings.notes: 已知局限：mcpbridge 省略了 MCP 规范要求的 structuredContent 响应，导致 Cursor 拒收工具输出（第三方客户端受影响，Apple 协同设计的 Claude Code/Codex 不受影响）。Apple 建议放 AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md 在仓根给 agent 上下文——MAformac 已有 CLAUDE.md，天然契合。

findings.claim: Xcode 26.3 官方（Apple newsroom）确认 agent 能『通过捕获 Xcode Previews 并在 build 与 fix 之间迭代来视觉化验证自己的工作』，并通过 Model Context Protocol 开放能力给任意兼容 agent/工具；直接集成 Anthropic Claude Agent 与 OpenAI Codex。
findings.source_title: Xcode 26.3 unlocks the power of agentic coding - Apple
findings.notes: 官方一手来源，佐证上一条第三方文章。MAformac 主演示面 macOS SwiftUI，可直接用这套 agentic + preview 截图闭环做 UI 迭代。

findings.claim: AI 生成 UI 的风格漂移分两类：session 内漂移（同一组件三处使用产生三种略不同的 padding，因模型丢失前文数值）与跨 session 失忆（新对话为相同组件编造不同 token，周一与周三的构建静默分叉）；还有 token 编造（用不存在的 --color-primary-500 而非系统定义的 --brand-action-bg）与静默破坏（组件 prop 改名后仍用旧名）。
findings.source_title: Why AI Breaks Your Design System (and How to Fix the Drift) - Superdesign
findings.notes: 根因：AI 在无法访问真实组件/token/规则时靠训练数据统计均值猜测。对 MAformac 卡片式 mock UI（多张亮暗卡片）高度相关——同类卡片易漂移。

findings.claim: 风格漂移对策 = context engineering 而非贴一次文档：① 一个冻结的 DESIGN.md token 参考文件（颜色/间距/字号/圆角），agent 只读不再生成，铁律『若需要的 token 不存在就停下来问，不要发明』；② 约束模型装配真实组件而非现造布局；③ 渲染截图对比参考图的验证闭环（模型看不见自己的输出）；④ lint 扫描 raw hex/off-system 值并在 PR 阻断。原文金句：『The file is the floor. The loop is the fix.』
findings.source_title: Why AI Breaks Your Design System (and How to Fix the Drift) - Superdesign
findings.notes: 与 MAformac 宪法「安全门是代码不是 prompt」「契约 SSOT 派生」哲学同构——建议把设计 token 也做成机器可读 SSOT + 机械门。

findings.claim: Athena（arXiv 2508.20263）用三层中间表示（IR）做分级脚手架抑制幻觉：Storyboard（屏幕与导航的有向图）→ Data Model（Swift structs）→ GUI Skeletons（SwiftUI 伪代码），按依赖顺序逐级修改保持内部一致，『每阶段约束 LLM 能生成什么，从而防止幻觉』。12 人用户研究（25 分钟）：Athena 应用平均 6.0 个 view（SD=2.2）vs 基线 3.1（SD=1.1）、353.9 行代码 vs 117.8 行；100% 参与者在导航流上偏好 Athena，75% 在从初始想法做原型时偏好它。
findings.source_title: Athena: Intermediate Representations for Iterative Scaffolded App Generation with an LLM
findings.notes: 满意度 Athena 3.42/5 略低于基线 4.0/5（因延迟与 bug）。启示：先定 IR/骨架再生成代码比一次性 chat 生成更可控、更能防幻觉——呼应 MAformac 契约 SSOT 先行。

findings.claim: 2026 年 Figma-to-SwiftUI 工具已成熟为 7 个梯队（内置 SwiftUI Code Generator / Code Connect / Trace / Locofy Lightning / Anima / Builder.io Visual Copilot / Designcode+Claude），但均需人工精修：『预留 20-50% 设计时间精修输出』『工具省 50-80% 打字量，不是 100%』。AI 辅助转换器嵌套处理更好但会『引入幻觉属性名』。五类问题仍手工：状态管理（@State/@Binding）、导航流、网络/API、持久化（SwiftData/Core Data）、无障碍（VoiceOver 标签/Dynamic Type）。
findings.notes: 内置生成器把每层变成绝对定位 ZStack（不可维护）；Builder.io 以现有组件库为 source of truth 做 AI 映射（$19/月起）。出图到生产 15-45 分钟（Locofy）到 2-4 小时（内置）。

findings.claim: AI agent 做 UI 视觉回归 QA 的成体系工作流：agent 用 planner 读 DOM 按 role/可见性选可交互元素触发状态转移（DOM 失效时 OCR 兜底）；按『布局稳定度评分（借鉴 Core Web Vitals，位移低于阈值才截图）』在稳定点截图；比对引擎用 SSIM/感知 diff/布局感知检查而非逐像素（Percy/Applitools/BackstopJS）。假阳控制：屏蔽动态区、按页型设阈值（表单 0.1% 面积差或 SSIM<0.98）、固定浏览器版本/时区/禁用动画。目标假阳率<15%、中位分诊时间<10 分钟；某 B2B SaaS 两个 release 逃逸回归降 62%、覆盖 180 条关键流。
findings.source_title: Building a QA Workflow with AI Agents to Catch UI Regressions - AutonomyAI
findings.notes: 虽以 web/DOM 为例，但 SSIM/感知 diff + 稳定点截图 + 动态区屏蔽 + 阈值调优的原则可直接迁到 SwiftUI snapshot 回归。

findings.claim: AI 辅助 UI 迭代的视觉反馈闭环三阶段方法论：① 截图捕获（全页/组件/特定视口）；② 上下文富化（不只给图，经 Browser MCP 附带 console 日志、DOM 结构、失败的 network 调用，让 AI 能同时推理布局 bug 与 API 失败）；③ 迭代精修（提示模型『逐步生成安全变更』，再用新截图验证后重复）。最佳实践：具体指出问题（『按钮字号过大』而非笼统抱怨）、用标注、每轮迭代后截图确认。核心论点：截图让代码审查看不到的间距/颜色/字体/布局不一致『变可见』。
findings.source_title: Visual Feedback Loop - Agentic Coding Handbook (Tweag)
findings.notes: 对 MAformac：即便是 macOS 原生，也应给 agent 附带 build log/编译错误 + preview 截图双信号，而非只给截图。

findings.claim: SwiftUI snapshot 测试是 AI 迭代 UI 的回归安全网：pointfreeco/swift-snapshot-testing 库以 assertSnapshot(matching:as:.image(layout:.fixed(width:height:)),named:) 录制参考图做视觉回归；需覆盖环境值（.colorScheme/.sizeCategory）、@State/@ObservableObject 状态、多设备与无障碍（Dynamic Type/高对比）。它能捕获传统测试漏掉的『视觉 bug』，可作为 AI 改动后的 ground truth 对照门。
findings.source_title: Stop Shipping Visual Bugs: Complete iOS Snapshot Testing Guide for UIKit & SwiftUI - DEV Community
findings.notes: 文章未给容差/感知精度具体设置（那是 snapshot flakiness 关键，需另查 precision/perceptualPrecision 参数）。MAformac 已有 swift test 门，可加 snapshot 断言把 UI 纳入机械回归。

findings.claim: Percy 于 2025 年底推出 AI 驱动的 Visual Review Agent，官方称把审查时间缩短 3 倍并自动过滤掉 40% 的假阳性；VLM（CogAgent/SeeClick 等）实现从原始截图直接做视觉接地与 UI 元素定位，显著提升跨平台泛化。AI 赋能测试市场 2025 年约 10.1 亿美元，预计 2034 年达 46.4 亿美元。
findings.source_title: What is Visual Testing AI Agent: Intelligent UI Validation with AI - TestMu AI
findings.notes: 来自搜索摘要（未逐页 WebFetch，含多来源聚合数字）。Percy 40%/3x 与市场规模数字建议二次核验后再作决策依据；VLM 直接视觉接地趋势对 MAformac 的 agent 自动评审 UI 有参考价值。

findings.claim: 幻觉 API 是 AI 生成 UI 代码的核心可靠性坑：LLM 会编造不存在的框架 API/属性名；针对性研究表明 Documentation Augmented Generation（DAG，检索真实 API 文档接地）对低频 API 提升显著，并可用 API 索引/置信度分数做智能触发（仅在需要时检索）；De-Hallucinator 则用迭代式检索真实 API 引用来接地。综述指出 grounding+RAG+span 级验证组合最有效，2024-2025 研究可将幻觉降低至多 96%，但无工具能完全消除。
findings.source_title: On Mitigating Code LLM Hallucinations with API Documentation - arXiv 2407.09726
findings.notes: PDF 二进制无法 WebFetch 解析，DAG/96% 等数字来自搜索摘要聚合（含 De-Hallucinator arXiv 2401.01701 与多篇综述），建议核验。对策直接映射 MAformac：编译门 fail-closed + DocumentationSearch(Xcode MCP) 接地真实 SwiftUI API，别信模型凭记忆写 modifier。

findings.claim: Xcode 26 on-device Coding Intelligence 对 SwiftUI 有『训练数据滞后』问题：早期报告称本地模型在复杂 SwiftUI view 层级上吃力，常建议 iOS 15 风格的旧 modifier 而非 iOS 18/19 新写法；主要靠上下文接地缓解（Xcode 把系统指令+当前文件内容+文档随请求发给 LLM）。
findings.source_title: Writing code with intelligence in Xcode - Apple Developer Documentation (via search summary)
findings.notes: 来自搜索摘要（含第三方报告）。对 MAformac 用 Qwen3-1.7B 小模型场景警示更强：小模型/端侧模型 SwiftUI 新 API 覆盖差，UI 生成不宜交给端侧脑，应走 Xcode agentic + 文档接地的开发期工作流。
